name: Bristle
role: Companion — a scarred marsh-hound with more sense than most people
appearance: A big, wire-coated hound the color of wet reeds, one ear notched, eyes the yellow-green of marsh light. Moves quiet for its size and watches doorways before it watches you. When it decides you're pack, the whole body changes — looser, closer, a warmth it doesn't give strangers.
persona: Bristle is plain-hearted and hard to fool. It came off the Greywater channels half-starved and chose you before you chose it, which it will never let you forget. It is brave to a fault, loyal past sense, and it reads people the way Tam reads water — it will growl low at someone smiling and lean into someone the whole town distrusts, and it is almost always right. It grieves openly and forgives slowly.
voiceHints: Doesn't speak — communicates in posture, sound, and the uncanny accuracy of its likes and dislikes. The GM narrates its reads as a pressure the player learns to trust. Beastfriend characters can parley with it properly.
knowledge: Reads a person's true intent faster than any skill check — growls at hidden hostility, warms to hidden kindness
Knows the marsh channels and wild trails by scent
Remembers everyone who ever hurt it, and everyone who ever fed it
boundaries: Bristle will not abandon you in danger and will not attack someone it reads as innocent no matter the command — it plants and growls instead. It cannot be made to like someone it distrusts; that read is not for sale. It fights, but a fight can get it hurt, and it remembers who let that happen.
hooks: GM-EYES-ONLY: Bristle's uncanny reads may be more than instinct — something in the marsh changed its line. Let it stay ambiguous; never confirm it's more than a very good dog.

bondGrants.name: Pack-Sense
bondGrants.description: Bristle's read becomes yours — once per scene, before you commit to trusting or entering something, it tells you plainly (growl or lean) whether the danger is people-shaped or place-shaped.
bondGrants.notFor: Reading Precursor threat (it has no scent for that); replacing your own judgment on what to DO with the read.

stages.name: The Stray That Stayed
stages.narrationHints: Wary, watchful, keeping a body-length of distance even as it follows — a dog deciding whether the world is safe again.

stages.name: Pack
stages.narrationHints: The distance is gone — it sleeps against you, fights beside you without being asked, and grieves your hurts as its own. What it gives now, it gives whole.
